body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: AcuRite isolated network data flow
body.intention: I intend that the isolated network daemon reliably captures weather data without regressing cloud sync or creating silent data loss.
body.description:
**What changed:** AcuRite device now sends data to isolated network segment with local daemon listener; data no longer routed through main network or cloud.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Device cannot reach actual myacurite.com (intentional isolation), so no cloud sync; if user expects cloud backup, this breaks that expectation
- dnsmasq misconfiguration could cause device to hang on DNS lookups
- CSV path hardcoded to /home/akien/AcuRite/weather.csv; if laptop path changes, dashboard breaks
- If device sends unexpected WU format variants (different field names), parser fails silently and data loss occurs
- Daemon crash → weather.csv stops updating; no alert to user

**Signals to watch:**
- CSV file growing at expected rate (new rows every ~20s per device cycle)
- weather.html dashboard showing live data without stale timestamps (>10min old for outdoor sensors)
- Daemon logs show expected request count and merge pattern (one request + one request = one CSV row)
- No errors in datacenter_logs/acurite_device/daemon.log

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-29 (14 days) or when device has been running for 24h continuous.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
